text:
Market today: Chip stocks are taking a hit today after Samsung's earnings missed high AI expectations, driving a sell-off across the sector. This is the main headwind for your tech-heavy book.

Your holdings: SOXL is a major concern at -37.21% and leveraged, especially with the chip sell-off. SMH (-9.41%) also needs attention. SPAL, a new leveraged position, is already down -22.15% and highly volatile. Margin amplifies these drops.

Watch: Keep a close eye on tomorrow's Fed minutes for potential market shifts. Also, watch for any follow-through on today's chip weakness.

Bottom line: Prioritize risk management on your leveraged and underperforming chip positions.
plays: Review SOXL's stop; consider reducing exposure given its deep loss and leveraged nature amidst chip weakness.
Trim SMH if it approaches its 540 stop, aligning with the "TRIM" status and sector headwinds.
Consider ZS for a new long at 150.94-150.79 with a stop at 150.56, based on a strong scanner setup.
